carbon_policy_signal.item: EU doubles down on carbon tax for international flights; expands EUA-like mechanisms to aviation sector.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Aviation carbon tax expansion raises EU carbon cost floor and signals sectoral broadening; indirect boost to power merit-order switching away from coal/gas via embedded carbon cost pass-through.

risk_flags: Storage at 35.57% (12th percentile, 12.5pp below seasonal) — refill pace into summer will set H2 thermal dispatch risk.
GB Power at 134.29 EUR/MWh (96th percentile) — tight interconnector capacity and Irish wind volatility driving premium; downside if renewables persist at 90th percentile.
Iran war and shadow fleet sanctions tighten Brent/LNG arb, but Portugal-style demand destruction and NATO renewables acceleration create offsetting structural headwind.
US pressure on EU methane enforcement reduces gas scarcity premium; weakens coal-to-gas switching incentive.

base.summary: Renewables ease off 90th percentile; storage refill continues gradual pace; GB-DE spread normalizes slightly as interconnector headroom returns.

downside.summary: Renewables sustain 90th percentile; Portugal-style demand destruction accelerates across EU; storage refill accelerates. Thermal margin compresses.

upside.summary: Shadow fleet sanctions broaden or Iran conflict escalates; LNG arb widens, Brent rallies. Storage refill slows; summer thermal call reasserts despite renewables.

themes: GB power premium and renewables suppression
Storage deficit and summer thermal upside
Geopolitical risk (Iran, shadow fleet sanctions) vs. demand destruction (Portugal fossil phase-out)
Carbon policy expansion (aviation EUA, methane softening)
top_drivers: GB Power at 96th percentile driven by tight interconnector capacity and Irish wind tail-risk, offset partly by renewables at 90th percentile suppressing overall DA merit order.
Storage deficit (12.5pp below seasonal) will determine summer thermal call intensity; currently masked by high renewables but refill pace is the pivot.

Geopolitical layering (Iran war, shadow fleet sanctions) pushes Brent and LNG arb, but EU demand destruction (Portugal fossil phase-out, NATO renewables push, methane enforcement softening) creates structural headwind to sustained price support.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 96th percentile amid 90th-percentile renewables; storage 12.5pp below seasonal average creates summer thermal call asymmetry despite Iran-war geopolitical risk.

watchlist.why: Currently 12.5pp below seasonal average — early summer refill trajectory will signal whether thermal call extends into H2 or renewables persistence caps upside.
GB at 96th percentile vs. DE at 72nd percentile — interconnector constraint and Irish wind volatility are the delta; watch for mean-reversion if GB renewables spike.

watchlist.metric: Shadow fleet sanctions scope and timeline (news-driven)
watchlist.why: EU implementation details will determine Brent-to-TTF LNG arb pass-through; Russia crude supply tightness supports energy cost floor but demand destruction offsets.

watchlist.metric: Portugal renewables capex and auction calendar (announcement-driven)
watchlist.why: Iran-war-triggered fossil phase-out signals EU demand destruction; timing and volume will shape H2 gas/power load destruction narrative.
Currently 62nd percentile (46.68 EUR/MWh), flat monthly — storage deficit should support summer floor, but methane enforcement softening and demand-side policy underpin ceiling.

watchlist_dated.name: EU methane rule enforcement coordination
watchlist_dated.why: US pressure ongoing; clarification on timeline and tariff escalation risk will reset gas supply scarcity premium.
